FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class ItemsViewController

Namespace
FluentGpu.Controls
Assembly
FluentGpu.Controls.dll

Imperative handle for an ItemsView (the WinUI methods that live on the control object: ItemsView.idl:46-58 — CurrentItemIndex, StartBringItemIntoView, and the selection API via Selection). Pass one to ItemsView.Create; the component wires it at mount.

public sealed class ItemsViewController
Inheritance
object
ItemsViewController
Inherited Members
object.Equals(object)
object.Equals(object, object)
object.GetHashCode()
object.GetType()
object.ReferenceEquals(object, object)
object.ToString()

Properties

CurrentItemIndex

WinUI CurrentItemIndex (idl:46-47, default −1) — the keyboard-current item.

public int CurrentItemIndex { get; }

Property Value

int

Selection

The live selection model — Select/Deselect/IsSelected/SelectAll/DeselectAll/InvertSelection (ItemsView.idl:53-58) are its methods; range-based, so they never realize items.

public SelectionModel? Selection { get; }

Property Value

SelectionModel

Methods

ScrollBy(float)

Nudge the virtualized viewport by delta DIP along its scroll axis (clamped). The drag-reorder EDGE AUTO-SCROLL seam: a composing list (ListView) calls this while the pointer drags near the viewport edge (the plan's E5-L3 edge auto-scroll in virtualized lists). No-op for non-virtual hosts.

public void ScrollBy(float delta)

Parameters

delta float

StartBringItemIntoView(int, float)

WinUI StartBringItemIntoView(index, BringIntoViewOptions) (idl:52): realizes the target by scrolling the virtualized viewport. alignmentRatio NaN = minimal scroll (the default BringIntoViewOptions); 0 = align item start to viewport start, 1 = end to end (the Home/End ratios, ItemsViewInteractions.cpp:1013-1016).

public void StartBringItemIntoView(int index, float alignmentRatio = NaN)

Parameters

index int
alignmentRatio float
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
